message: Node started: Perform a discrete combinatorial analysis of the intersection probability for two chords. First, list all possible ways to assign the four endpoints of two chords to the four available quadrants, given the constraint that for each chord, its two endpoints must reside in different quadrants. Second, for every unique configuration of these two chords, determine if they must intersect, might intersect, or cannot intersect. Third, calculate the exact probability of intersection by treating all valid endpoint assignments as equally likely within the defined sample space. Derive this result from first principles.
